MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Cilley for Laurent Reboul.

Mr. Cilley provided background histroy on the matter, spoke of Edith's trust and Olga's trust, and presented oral argument to support his position. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Rosada/A. Payne.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Nov-25-2009 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Stacey Nelson for Trustee of the Claude Reboul Trust. James Cilley for Laurent Reboul (Beneficiary). Richard Ehrman for Wells Fargo Bank (Petitioner).

Mr. Ehrman stated that he did not know why Mr. Reidy did not transfer the funds from the original trust to the Edith trust, that it was a matter of form over substance and that they took over on an "as is" basis. In addition, he stated that there was a lawsuit in the Lindemood Trust and that there would be significant consequences. Mr. Cilley stated that both Beneficiaries consented to the Edith trust not being established and that there was no advantage to the Beneficiaries to create the Edith trust. Ms. Nelson took no position. Counsel proceeded to present oral argument to support their positions with regard to consolidation of the Lafayette property with the pending civil action, validity of the documents relating to the Lafayette property, distribution of the income principal and payment of litigation fees. The Court directed Mr. Ehrman to submit a proposed order. The Court will take the matter under submission as of the date the order is received.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Petition For Fees To Attys For Former Successor Trustee And For Order Creating Reserve
Off Calendar Jun-03-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with Case #464270, Laurent Reboul vs. Valley of California, Inc.

APPEARANCES: Amy Harrington and John Boessenecker for Respondent Edward Reidy. Richard Ehrman for Wells Fargo Bank. Carlena Tapella for Lisa Berg. James Anderson for Laurent Reboul. Robert Luther for Valley of California, Inc.

The Court commences an evidentiary hearing. Respective counsel present arguments on the motions before the Court.

The Court orders Wells Fargo Bank to set aside $150,000 which shall be deposited into a segregated account to pay Respondent Edward Reidy's attorney's fees and costs incurred up to the date of filing the Petition. Before any payments are made Edward Reidy must file a Petition for fees & costs supported by evidence which the Court may review in-camera and redact if necessary or requested. Counsel may petition the Court for fees incurred subsequent to November 21, 2008 which shall be heard on May 13, 2009 at 1:30 pm in Department 204. Counsel on behalf of Edward Reidy to prepare an order reflecting the Court's ruling and circulate for approval of all parties.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR #428. Clerk: Nicole Davis/Jackie Alameda.

Petition For Fees To Attys For Former Successor Trustee And For Order Creating Reserve
Off Calendar Mar-18-2009 Continued To Apr-02-09 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 210
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 464270 Laurent Reboul v Valley of California.

Appearances: Richard Erhman for Wells Fargo Bank. Carlena Tapella appeared by telephone for Lisa Berg. James Anderson for Laurent Reboul. John Boessenecker and Amy Harrington for Ed Reidy.

The Court informed all Parties that it would be helpful for them to provide the equivalent of a Case Management Conference (CMC) statement. The Parties informed the Court that they agreed to participate in mediation before Judge Rebecca Westerfield from JAMS. The Parties presented case history for the Court and oral argument to support positions with regard to Ms. Harrington's request to set aside $100,000.00 for the Attorney's litigation fees. This issue was set for trial/evidentiary hearing and is to be heard on April 2, 2009 at 1:30 p.m. The location of the Courtroom is to be determined. The Parties will submit their CMC's on April 2, 2009. The Parties are to file any papers to support their positions and provide copies the the Clerk two days prior to April 2, 2009. The Parties are to report the status of the mediation on that date.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley, CSR 225. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/ Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy R. Lee.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #7, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-07-2009 Continued To Jan-14-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Boessenecker for Edward Reidy (Respondent). Mark Schmuck on behalf of James Cilley for Laurent Reboul.

Mr. Boessenceker noted that there were several matters pending, stated that he and Mr. Cilley had spoken about mediation and requested the Court order the matter to mediation to be completed within 45 days. Mr. Schmuck stated that he had no discussion regarding mediation with Mr. Cilley and that he had no problem with the matter being heard in June with the other accountings or continued to a later date. The matter was continued to January 14, 2009. The Parties were directed to discuss the possibility of mediation and to contact the Examiner with regard to the status.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Jackie Alameda/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Bobby Mann.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-22-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amy Harrington for Edward Reidy (Trustee). James Cilley for Laurent Reboul (Objector).

Mr. Cilley stated that pursuant to the stipulation signed in February, that all accountings for the entire administration were to be heard at the same time and requested the Court issue an order for the same. Ms. Harrington argued that the order was not specific, that the accountings covering 1996 through 2001 were different from the remaining accountings and that it was already set for trial. The Court stated that all the accountings filed and on calendar through October 27, 2008 should be heard together. All matters on calendar upto October 27, 2008 were referred to the Department of the Presiding Judge, (order for the same signed in open court).

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-09-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number 464270 (Laurent Reboul vs Valley of California).

Appearances: James Cilley for Laurent Reboul. Amy Harrington for Edward Reidy (Successor Trustee). Carlena Tapella appeared by telephone for Lisa Berg.

Mr Cilley stated that the order consolidating all matters except for the accounting had been filed. In addition, he stated that he had objected to the accounting, that he had not received the accounting for 2007 and that he would be objecting to all future accountings. Ms. Harrington stated that the Mr. Cilley and his client's litigation matter had to be resolved before the accounting could be approved. Ms. Tapella stated that she objected to the first portion of the accounting and that there would also be objections to all future accountings. Mr. Cilley requested an evidentiary hearing be set on all of the accountings and stated that it would be a 5 day trial. The Court referred the accountings on the trust matter to the Presiding Judge's office to be set for trial, (signing the order for the same in open court). The Petition To Suspend Powers on the trust matter and the civil matter were continued to February 17, 2009 for status.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.

MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number 464270 (Reboul vs Valley of California).

Appearances: James Cilley for Laurent Reboul. Paul Kelly and John Boessenecker for Edward Reidy.

Mr. Boesseneker requested a continuance stating that Mr. Cassolas needed more time to do the appraisal and that Ms. Harrington needed more time to complete the accounting. Mr. Cilley agreed to additional time and stated that if the accounting was not filed, that he would be requesting the suspension of Mr. Reidy. The matter was continued to August 19, 2008.

Petition To Suspend Powers Of Trustee And Appoint Temporary Trustee Pending Hearing For Removal Of Trustee Petition To Remove Trustee And Appoint Successor
Off Calendar Apr-22-2008 Continued To Jul-15-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Cilley for Petitioner. Amy Harrington and Paul Kelly for Ed Reidy (Trustee).

Mr. Cilley stated that the case was not ready to be set for trial, requested a 60 day continuance and stated that he needed the accounting in order to move forward. Ms. Harrington stated that they had filed 5 years of accounting, that real property was acquired but that it had not been evaluated, and requested a 90 day continuance. Mr. Silly stated that Mr. Reidy was witholding distribution and that he would file an ex parte petition for suspension of powers. Ms. Harrington stated that Mr. Reidy had made distribution, had filed his petitions and that there was no retaliation on any level. The matter was continued to July 15, 2008. The Court ordered the accounting be filed before that date.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Cilly for Laurent Reboul (Petitioner). Amy Harrington for Ed Reidy (Trustee).

Mr. Cilly reported that the parties had reached a stipulation and read the terms for the record. Ms. Harrington stipulated. The Court continued all matters to April 22, 2008 for further status. The accounting by Mr. Reidy is to be filed before March 18, 2008. Mr. Cilly will prepare the stipulation and order.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR# 11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Lee.
